title: Setting your heart ablaze — Tesla vs Edison
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line aphorism note from October-2021: "Setting your heart ablaze. Unrelenting conviction. Tesla vs Edison. Find great people around you. Escapism?"
  • The Tesla vs Edison frame — the Tesla (genius-but-uncommercial) vs Edison (executor-who-won) archetype dichotomy — first surfaces here, becoming a recurring lens in Yahir's founder archetype thinking.
  • "Unrelenting conviction" is the max effort commit frame phrased as a mood — the disposition behind execution, not just the execution itself.
  • Closing question — "Escapism?" — Yahir self-interrogating whether the manifesto-mode is itself a substitute for action; the self suspicion built into the form.
  • Companion to the same-week Buy a Tesla aspiration — Tesla the man and Tesla the car appear within five days; the founder-archetype and the consumer-aspiration are inseparable in Yahir's frame.

Note

Five lines, four assertions, one self-doubting question mark. Yahir at 18 collapsing the tesla vs edison dichotomy, the conviction disposition, the surround yourself with great people move, and the meta-question about whether all this self-talk is itself a dodge. The "escapism?" line is the part that ages best — Yahir already aware that the can become a closed loop where writing-about-doing displaces doing. The Tesla-vs-Edison invocation is the kind of distant-mentor reach that defines the late-2021 cluster: pulling historical figures into the present-tense decision frame.

Connections

  • tesla vs edison · nikola tesla · thomas edison
  • max effort commit · fear of not doing
  • 2021 10 24 buy a tesla — Tesla-the-car companion
